data.title: Polestar reports record retail sales in 2025
data.description:
• Polestar (PSNY) retail sales amounted to an estimated 15,608 cars in Q4 2025, up 27% versus Q4 2024, the company said on Friday.
  • For the full year, retail sales totalled approximately 60,119 cars, a growth of 34% compared to 2024.
  • “For retail sales volumes, 2025 has been the best year ever for Polestar, despite continued external headwinds and challenging market conditions," Michael Lohscheller, Polestar CEO said.
  • "We are gaining market share and outselling many established car brands across key European markets – a testament to the expansion of our sales network, which has grown by over 50% this year, our attractive model line-up and the team’s hard work.”
  • Shares in Polestar Automotive Holding UK PLC (PSNYW) were up ~8% in premarket trading.

data.title: Bruker secures $500M in multi-year MRI superconductor orders
data.description:
• The Bruker Energy & Supercon Technologies division, a segment of Bruker (BRKR), on Friday announced two multi-year supply agreements for Bruker’s latest superconductors, which are used by global radiology companies to build their next generations of magnetic resonance imaging magnets.
  • The combined order value of the two agreements is approximately $500 million in expected future BEST revenues, with different agreement duration periods, with one agreement extending up to seven years.
  • These recently concluded agreements represent expansions of previous superconductor supply framework agreements.
  • Bruker (BRKR) up 1.8% premarket at $53.41.

data.title: MGM Resorts is seen by Texas Capital as poised for a breakout year
data.description: Texas Capital Bank started off coverage on MGM Resorts International (MGM) with a Buy rating.Analyst David Bain and his team think MGM (MGM) will benefit from improved Las Vegas investor sentiment and fundamentals in 2026."We believe current low-to-mid-tier Las Vegas leisure softness is well known and even reflected in a few recently reduced sell-side estimates," noted Bain. He also pointed to the strong group calendar, fiscal stimulus/tax rebates, thawing of international travel, MGM Grand remodel completion, and easier revenue comparisons as potential catalysts.The positive view on MGM (MGM) also envisions omnichannel unlocking and momentum in Macau. On the capital allocation front, a share buyback re-acceleration is expected following MGM's (MGM) application withdrawal of a $2.3B casino project license in Yonkers, New York, $546M sale of Northfield Park operations, and general cash flow and cash contributions from BetMGM and MGM China.As another wildcard for investors to consider, Bain pointed out that plans for Japan’s first integrated casino resort are being overlooked. "While a project opening ~4 years from now should carry a discounted value, we believe the potential return dynamic for Japan’s first integrated casino resort is understated/underappreciated. MGM’s share of Japan’s EBITDAM could exceed $800M, in our estimation," he wrote.Texas Capital Bank assigned a price target of $56 to MGM Resorts (MGM) to represent more than 50% upside potential.

data.title: Meta strikes three big nuclear power deals to support AI ambitions; Vistra, Oklo shares surge
data.description:
Meta (META) unveiled three agreements Friday to secure up to 6.6 GW of nuclear power by 2035 for its data centers that are among the most sweeping and ambitious so far between tech companies and nuclear power providers.

Meta (META) announced a deal with Vistra (VST) to purchase electricity from three existing nuclear plants in Ohio and Pennsylvania, and will back new small modular reactor projects that nuclear developers Oklo (OKLO) and TerraPower are planning to build over the next decade; the deals follow an agreement announced in June to obtain energy from a Constellation Energy nuclear site.

Vistra (VST) +14.2% and Oklo (OKLO) +17% pre-market.

Meta's (META) 20-year agreements to buy power from Vistra's (VST) Perry and Davis-Besse plants in Ohio and Beaver Valley plant in Pennsylvania will provide more than 2,600 MW of energy, including 2,176 MW of operating generation beginning later this year and an additional 433 MW of combined power output increases, which Vistra said will comprise the largest nuclear uprates supported by a corporate customer in the U.S.

Vistra (VST) said the PPAs provide certainty to pursue subsequent license renewals for each of the reactors, which would extend each license an additional 20 years; currently, Beaver Valley Unit 1 is licensed through 2036, Davis-Besse is licensed through 2037, Perry is licensed through 2046, and Beaver Valley Unit 2 is licensed through 2047.

Oklo (OKO) said its agreement with Meta (META) advances its plans to develop a 1.2 GW power campus in Pike County, Ohio, and provides a mechanism for Meta to prepay for power and provide funding to advance project certainty for Oklo's Aurora powerhouse deployment; financial terms were not disclosed.

Meta (META) also will help fund TerraPower's development of two reactors to generate up to 690 MW of power as early as 2032, and provides Meta with rights for energy from as many as six other TerraPower reactors by 2035; financial terms were not disclosed.

Meta's (META) new deals follow CEO Mark Zuckerberg's plans to invest hundreds of billions of dollars through the end of the decade on AI and the infrastructure needed to support it.

data.title: Kroger sells its Vitacost business as part of a strategic review
data.description:
The Kroger Co. (KR) announced on Friday that it sold its Vitacost subsidiary to iHerb for an undisclosed amount. The transaction is not expected to impact the company's previously issued financial guidance for 2025. The transaction closed on January 8.

Vitacost is a pure-play health and wellness e-commerce business that formed in the mid-1990s as a mail-order vitamin catalog and was acquired by Kroger (KR) in 2014 for about $280 million (51% premium) to bolster the company's digital and ship-to-home capabilities. Vitacost has remained an online-focused brand within Kroger's (KR) portfolio, serving as a national platform for vitamins, supplements, and natural/organic products

Kroger (KR) CEO Ron Sargent said the sale is an important step in the grocery store operator's plan to review all non-core assets to determine their ongoing contribution and role within the company.

Shares of Kroger (KR) dipped 0.1% in premarket action. Shares have managed only a meager 1.5% gain over the last 52 weeks.

data.title: KalVista reports $49M in 2025 Ekterly revenue, shares rise
data.description:
• KalVista Pharmaceuticals (KALV) reported approximately $35 million in global net product revenue from Ekterly (sebetralstat) in the fourth quarter and about $49 million for full-year 2025, sending shares 14% higher in morning trade.
  • “We are extremely pleased with our performance since launching Ekterly in July, which reflects steady execution, growing utilization, and continued momentum across our business,” said Ben Palleiko, CEO of KalVista (KALV).
  • “Fundamental demand has remained strong, with some effects from seasonal variability, and we are encouraged by the continued high level of interest in switching to Ekterly."
  • Internationally, KalVista Pharmaceuticals (KALV) said it was seeing strong uptake in Germany, with early prescribing behaviors mirroring the positive trends it observed in the US, and expects to expand into other major markets during 2026.

data.title: Southwest Airlines rallies to 52-week high after double upgrade from JPMorgan
data.description:
Southwest Airlines Co. (LUV) jumped in early trading on Friday after JPMorgan lifted the airline stock to an Overweight rating in a double upgrade from its prior ratings stance of Underweight.

Notably, analyst Jamie Baker and his team believe the potential for 2026 $5 EPS guidance from Southwest to be "attractively probable." That EPS guide would smash the consensus estimate of $3.03 for the year and be likely to push shares higher, according to Baker.

Southwest Airlines (LUV) was highlighted as possessing the airline industry’s deepest track record of profitability, an investment-grade balance sheet, and a loyal customer base. After operational hiccups and procrastination to adapt pressured the share price and investors’ patience, Baker and his team now believe the core business is starting to stabilize and believe initiatives (bag fees and seat assignments) are progressing as planned, as evidenced by management reiterating incremental EBIT targets throughout 2025, despite the demand headwinds in the industry. 

JPMorgan assigned a price target of $60 to Southwest Airlines (LUV).

Shares of Southwest Airlines (LUV) were up 3.8% in premarket action to $44.55 to mark a new 52-week high.

data.title: Mainstreet Equity doubles dividen to CAD 0.08
data.description:
• Mainstreet Equity Corp. (MEQ:CA) declares CAD 0.08/share quarterly dividend, 100% increase from prior dividend of CAD 0.04.
  • Forward yield 0.13%
  • Payable Jan. 31; for shareholders of record Jan. 16; ex-div Jan. 16.
  • See MEQ:CA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Ucommune gets Nasdaq notice for non-compliance
data.description:
• Ucommune International (UK) received written notification from the staff, indicating that for the last 34 consecutive business days, the closing bid price for the company's share was below the minimum bid price of $1.00 per share.
  • The Nasdaq notification letter has no current effect on the listing or trading of the company's shares.
  • The company may be eligible for an additional 180-day compliance period.

data.title: Liquidia reports $148.3M in 2025 YUTREPIA sales
data.description:
• Liquidia (LQDA) on Friday announced preliminary, unaudited full-year 2025 net product sales of YUTREPIA™ (treprostinil) inhalation powder, estimating $90.1 million in Q4 and $148.3 million for the full year, while also providing updates on its commercial launch and clinical development pipeline.

    It received more than 2,800 unique patient prescriptions since launch in June 2025, and generated more than $30 million of positive cash flow during the fourth quarter 2025.
